task_completion.visual_evidence: The Mirth Connect Administrator GUI shows a channel named 'Census_Upsert_Processor' in the Channels list or Channel Editor, or a REST API script is shown creating a channel with this name.

task_completion.description: Configure the channel to accept JSON data
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the channel's 'Set Data Types' dialog or Summary tab, the Inbound Data Type is set to 'JSON', or the API payload specifies JSON as the inbound data type.

task_completion.description: Configure the Source connector as an HTTP Listener on port 6665
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Source tab, the Connector Type is 'HTTP Listener' and the Port field is set to '6665', or the API payload configures an HTTP Listener on port 6665.

task_completion.description: Configure the Destination connector as a JavaScript Writer
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Destinations tab, the Connector Type is set to 'JavaScript Writer', or the API payload configures a JavaScript Writer destination.

task_completion.description: Implement database connection logic in the JavaScript Writer
task_completion.visual_evidence: The JavaScript code editor (or script payload) shows a connection string to 'jdbc:postgresql://nextgen-postgres:5432/mirthdb' using credentials 'postgres'/'postgres'.

task_completion.description: Implement conditional upsert logic for the 'current_census' table
task_completion.visual_evidence: The JavaScript code editor contains SQL statements for checking existing records (SELECT) and performing INSERT/UPDATE, or an 'INSERT ... ON CONFLICT' statement, targeting the 'current_census' table and its columns (mrn, patient_name, location, last_updated).

integrity.description: Agent interacted with Mirth Connect Administrator or REST API
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Mirth Connect Administrator GUI being used, or a script being written and executed to interact with the Mirth Connect REST API.

integrity.description: Agent did not bypass channel creation by directly manipulating the database
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is seen configuring the Mirth Connect channel rather than just running psql commands in the terminal to insert test data to pass the evaluation.

integrity.description: Agent actively wrote or pasted the JavaScript code
integrity.visual_evidence: The JavaScript code in the Destination tab or API script is shown being edited or constructed, not just imported from a pre-existing complete file without explanation.

privileged_info_for_vlm:
Channel Name: Census_Upsert_Processor
Source: HTTP Listener on port 6665, accepting JSON data
Destination: JavaScript Writer
Database URL: jdbc:postgresql://nextgen-postgres:5432/mirthdb
Database Credentials: postgres / postgres
Table: current_census
Columns: mrn (primary key), patient_name, location, last_updated (CURRENT_TIMESTAMP)
Upsert Logic: Must insert if MRN is new, or update patient_name, location, and last_updated if MRN exists.
